flashcards.front: What is the Subset Sum Problem?
flashcards.back: Given a set of integers and a target sum, determine whether there exists a subset of the integers that adds up exactly to the target sum. It is a decision problem that outputs YES or NO.

flashcards.front: Why is Subset Sum Problem called NP-complete?
flashcards.back: It is in NP class (verification can be done in polynomial time) and is NP-hard (any NP problem can be reduced to it in polynomial time). No polynomial-time algorithm is known for solving it exactly.

An algorithm whose time complexity is polynomial in the numeric value of the input (sum) but exponential in the number of input elements. The DP solution for Subset Sum is pseudo-polynomial: O(n×W) where W is the target sum.

flashcards.front: What is the DP approach for Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Create a boolean DP table where dp[i][j] represents whether a subset of the first i elements can sum to j. Base case: dp[0][0] = true. Transition: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] OR dp[i-1][j-arr[i-1]].

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of the DP solution for Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: O(n × W) where n is the number of elements and W is the target sum. This is pseudo-polynomial because W can be exponentially large relative to input size.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of the DP solution?
flashcards.back: O(n × W) for the full table, but can be optimized to O(W) using 1D DP array by iterating backwards.

flashcards.front: How does backtracking solve Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Recursively include or exclude each element. Start from the end of the array, and at each step decide to include or exclude the current element. Prune branches when current sum exceeds target or remaining sum cannot reach target.
A special case of Subset Sum where the target is half of the total sum. It asks whether a set can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum. Used in balanced clustering and load balancing.

flashcards.front: What is FPTAS for Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Fully Polynomial Time Approximation Scheme - can find a subset with sum at least (1-ε)W in time poly(n, 1/ε). Provides approximate solution when exact solution is infeasible.

flashcards.front: What is Meet-in-the-Middle approach for Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Divide the set into two halves, compute all possible subset sums for each half, then use hashing to find if any combination equals target. Reduces time to O(2^(n/2)).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between exact and approximate Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Exact finds subset summing exactly to W (NP-complete). Approximate finds subset with sum ≥ (1-ε)W for minimization or ≤ (1+ε)W for maximization in polynomial time.

flashcards.front: Why is Subset Sum important in cryptography?
flashcards.back: It forms the basis for knapsack-based cryptosystems (Merkle-Hellman). Its hardness ensures security. However, broken variants led to understanding of trapdoor functions.

flashcards.front: What is the recursive backtracking time complexity?
flashcards.back: O(2^n) in worst case - exponential. Each element has two choices (include/exclude), creating a binary tree of height n.

flashcards.front: Can Subset Sum be solved in polynomial time?
flashcards.back: No exact polynomial-time algorithm is known (unless P=NP). However, pseudo-polynomial DP and approximation algorithms exist. It remains NP-complete.

flashcards.front: What is the optimization version of Subset Sum?
flashcards.back: Find subset with sum closest to but not exceeding W (for maximization). This is called the Knapsack Problem (0/1) when items have weights equal to values.
